title: ocotea notata
intro: Ocotea notata, also known as Mountain Pepper, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and compounds such as eugenol and beta-caryophyllene. The health benefits of Ocotea notata include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve pain and inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Ocotea notata can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or oil, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
intro_study: According to "Natural product research", Ocotea notata has significant antioxidant benefits, as its extracts and fractions showed effective antioxidant activity, with the ethyl acetate partition having a higher ORAC potential than the standard Ginkgo biloba.
